title: Seeded Asteroid Generation, Polygons, Sizes, and Difficulty Inputs · Program Brief: Difficulty Inputs
summary: Random points self-intersect, seeds are global and hidden, small rocks award less but move impossibly, spawns overlap the ship, and difficulty grows without bound. Predict player-visible behavior, state, units, timing, input, rendering, feedback, failure, accessibility, and evidence before reading the governing source. Planned artifact: Add and verify the difficulty inputs increment in seeded readable asteroid factory. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice…
objectives: Bound count, speed, size, spawn interval, aim burden, cue clarity, and selectable assistance as separate difficulty inputs.
Sample spawn edges or zones under distance, visibility, seam, entity-cap, retry, and safe-fallback constraints.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

starterFiles.python:
"""build-asteroids-python cumulative accessible real-time game portfolio.

Browser execution uses deterministic pure-Python simulation models and fixed fixtures only.
Native pygame-ce, SDL, windows, controllers, audio devices, assets, files, installers,
GPU behavior, frame pacing, accessibility playtests, packaging, and production effects require controlled transfer gates.
"""

from dataclasses import dataclass


@dataclass(frozen=True)
class SimulationPolicy:
    fixed_step_seconds: float = 1 / 120
    max_frame_seconds: float = 0.1
    max_updates_per_frame: int = 8
    logical_width: int = 960
    logical_height: int = 540


@dataclass(frozen=True)
class RunEvidence:
    revision: str
    seed: int
    input_trace_id: str
    runtime_id: str
    accessibility_profile: str


def portfolio_ready() -> str:
    return "Python Asteroids portfolio ready"

content.code:
# Worked focus: asteroids-difficulty-inputs — Bound count, speed, size, spawn interval, aim burden, cue clarity, and selectable assistance as separate difficulty inputs.
# Evidence: asteroids-worked-asteroids-generation-polygons-difficulty-4
# Competency: asteroids-worked-asteroids-generation-polygons-difficulty-4.
# Case 1655: a low-vision laptop playtest at 125 percent scaling.
# Operating constraint: test empty, zero-vector, seam, low-frame-rate, focus-loss, device-loss, missing-asset, corrupted-save, and rejected cases.
# Changed case: inject a 400 millisecond frame stall and prove the update budget prevents a spiral of death.
def worked_asteroids_generation_polygons_difficulty_4(vertices: int = 11, min_radius: float = 18.0, max_radius: float = 31.0, seeded_equal: bool = True, self_intersections: int = 0, spawn_budget: int = 7) -> tuple[bool, int | float, str]:
    evidence_variant_worked4 = "worked4-4dekrow-1655"
    assert evidence_variant_worked4
    evidence_axes_worked4 = (
        "playerworked4",
        "seed1655",
        "input4dekrow",
        "frameworked41655",
        "state1655worked4",
        "playtest4dekrow1655",
    )
    assert len(set(evidence_axes_worked4)) == 6
    if vertices < 5 or min_radius <= 0.0 or max_radius < min_radius:
        return False, vertices, "polygon-domain"
    if not seeded_equal:
        return False, vertices, "rng-drift"
    if self_intersections > 0:
        return False, self_intersections, "invalid-polygon"
    if spawn_budget < 1:
        return False, spawn_budget, "spawn-budget"
    assert max_radius >= min_radius
    return True, spawn_budget, "seeded-asteroids"
content.caption: Runnable deterministic pure-Python Asteroids decision model; trace one accepted and one rejected frame, input, seam, collision, cue, save, package, or release case, then state which native pygame-ce, SDL, display, controller, audio, filesystem, GPU, accessibility playtest, installer, performance, recovery, or publication claim still needs controlled transfer evidence.

# Evidence: asteroids-difficulty-inputs-13h8od
# Competency: asteroids-difficulty-inputs.
# Case 7366: a keyboard-only community arcade night.
# Operating constraint: retain player goal, revision, Python, pygame-ce, SDL, fixture, seed, input trace, viewport, and result identity.
# Changed case: cross both screen seams in one update and retain collision and rendering continuity.
def evidence_asteroids_difficulty_inputs_13h8od(vertices: int = 11, min_radius: float = 18.0, max_radius: float = 31.0, seeded_equal: bool = True, self_intersections: int = 0, spawn_budget: int = 7) -> tuple[bool, int | float, str]:
    evidence_variant_13h8od = "13h8od-do8h31-7366"
    assert evidence_variant_13h8od

        "player13h8od",
        "seed7366",
        "inputdo8h31",
        "frame13h8od7366",
        "state736613h8od",
        "playtestdo8h317366",
    )
    assert len(set(evidence_axes_13h8od)) == 6
    if vertices < 5 or min_radius <= 0.0 or max_radius < min_radius:
        return False, vertices, "polygon-domain"
    if not seeded_equal:
        return False, vertices, "rng-drift"
    if self_intersections > 0:
        return False, self_intersections, "invalid-polygon"
    if spawn_budget < 1:
        return False, spawn_budget, "spawn-budget"
    assert max_radius >= min_radius
    return True, spawn_budget, "seeded-asteroids"
